---
title: "DOTS & ECS"
---

Composition is where primitives combine into systems, and where the subtle
failures live. These are not bugs in your logic; they are mismatches between the
query you wrote and the query you meant.

## Enableable components: the four queries

{{< unity-type IEnableableComponent >}} separates *present* from *enabled*. Four
query builders address the four states, and choosing the wrong one is the most
common "stuck visible" bug.

| Builder | Matches | Use for |
|---|---|---|
| `WithAll<T>()` | present, enabled or disabled | structural presence only |
| `WithPresent<T>()` | present and enabled | currently active semantics |
| `WithDisabled<T>()` | present but disabled | the off set |
| `WithAbsent<T>()` | not present at all | the missing set |

::: {.gotcha title="WithAll ignores enabled state"}
`WithAll<T>()` matches an entity that has `T` regardless of whether `T` is
enabled. If you meant "currently active," you wanted `WithPresent<T>()`. This
single confusion is the number-one cause of entities that refuse to disappear.
:::

```{.wrong}
foreach (var t in SystemAPI.Query<RefRW<Visible>>().WithAll<Visible>()) { }
```

```{.right}
foreach (var t in SystemAPI.Query<RefRW<Visible>>().WithPresent<Visible>()) { }
```

## Transforms are three components

{{< unity-type LocalToParent >}} is gone. Hierarchy and placement are now
{{< unity-type Parent >}}, {{< unity-type LocalTransform >}}, and
{{< unity-type LocalToWorld >}}. Authoring whose runtime system queries
`LocalTransform` must use `TransformUsageFlags.Dynamic`; `None` can strip
`LocalTransform` and the query then returns zero entities.

```{.exec name="Confirm a transform query is non-empty" usings="Unity.Entities,Unity.Transforms"}
var em = World.DefaultGameObjectInjectionWorld.EntityManager;
var q = em.CreateEntityQuery(typeof(LocalTransform));
var n = q.CalculateEntityCount();
return n > 0 ? $"OK: {n}" : "FAIL: TransformUsageFlags likely None";
```

## ISystem is invisible to world.Systems

{{< unity-type ISystem >}} structs are unmanaged and do not appear in
`world.Systems`. Verifying a system "ran" by scanning that list will always
fail for unmanaged systems. Verify by effect: query the entities the system
should have changed.

```{.exec name="Verify by effect, not by system list" usings="Unity.Entities"}
var em = World.DefaultGameObjectInjectionWorld.EntityManager;
var q = em.CreateEntityQuery(typeof(LocalToWorld));
return "world entities touched by transforms: " + q.CalculateEntityCount();
```

System-entity components, if you must query them, require
`EntityQueryOptions.IncludeSystems`.

## readonly struct forbids object initializers

A `readonly struct` requires every field `readonly`, and `readonly` fields
cannot be assigned through an object initializer. If you need the initializer,
drop `readonly` from the struct; do not fight the compiler with reflection.

```{.wrong}
public readonly struct Config { public readonly int N; }
var c = new Config { N = 4 };
```

```{.right}
public struct Config { public int N; }
var c = new Config { N = 4 };
```

## GetSingleton needs RequireForUpdate

Every `GetSingleton<T>()` in `OnUpdate` needs a matching
`state.RequireForUpdate<T>()` in `OnCreate`. Without it the singleton may not
exist on the frame you read it, and the crash is latent — it appears only when
timing shifts.
